behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local module handoff (diff parser -> review-input builder). MOCK-MISS: unit tests for parser and formatter can both pass while wiring still forwards full-file blobs. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; fixture diffs are sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: a unit test of only the slicer would miss an integration bug where caller passes original file text instead of hunks. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is only about scoping input lines, not real API behavior or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PR-reading and input-construction modules together with a synthetic PR diff fixture containing large unchanged regions; assert emitted review payload contains only changed hunks/line ranges.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real GitHub diff-position semantics and HTTP API behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked comment API can accept incorrect position fields that real GitHub rejects or anchors incorrectly. REAL-INFRA: requires real network/API and PR context. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: integration with fake API misses off-by-one/side-specific position mapping that only appears in real diff view.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed positions, then fetch PR review comments from GitHub and verify each anchors to the intended file/hunk/line in the rendered diff.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment (defect detection quality). Deterministic stubs always pass and cannot validate whether the model actually finds the bug. REAL-INFRA alone is not the key boundary; LLM-DEP is. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: integration/system test with canned findings cannot catch regression where model stops recognizing the known bug pattern. Rejected Workflow because behavior text asks for a single outcome (identify a defect), not multi-agentic-step handoff correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run analysis with the real LLM on a seeded PR containing a known bug; assert at least one returned finding correctly references the seeded defect location/category.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module state-consistency check (inline-post responses -> summary assembler). No real OS/network/model output is required to expose mapping bugs. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: unit tests of summary formatting alone miss run-scoping bug where IDs from a prior run leak into current summary. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is ID correspondence logic from same run, not infra or model-quality dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Execute run-orchestrator components in-process with stubbed comment-post responses carrying generated IDs; assert every summary entry references an ID returned in that same execution context.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in local pipeline handoff/aggregation (finding list -> summary payload). MOCK-MISS: single-function tests can pass while orchestration drops items during transformation/filtering. No mandatory real OS/network/model dependency for the drop detection itself. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: unit test of serializer misses bug in upstream batching step that truncates findings before serializer runs. Rejected higher levels because behavior text targets payload completeness prior to posting.
behaviors.test_description: Wire finding collector, transformer, and summary builder with a multi-item fixture (including edge cases like long text/severity mix); assert cardinality and IDs match exactly between inline findings and final summary payload.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real pull request and real posting sequence over HTTP. With fixed findings, LLM output quality is excluded, so Agentic is not required. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: integration with mocked GitHub client misses real API constraints/order issues (commit SHA mismatch, review thread creation failures, rate-limit/validation responses). Rejected Workflow because behavior text fixes findings (no agentic decision chain).
behaviors.test_description: Run the automation against a real sandbox PR using precomputed findings; verify diff read succeeds, all inline comments are posted, and top-level summary is posted with expected references.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end agent pipeline combining real model generation with downstream posting/state transitions. MULTI-STEP: analyze (agentic) then system actions (inline + summary) with dependency handoffs. MOCK-HIDE: replacing model or API cannot expose failures where model output shape/quality breaks later stages or retry/replan logic. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Agentic-only test of analysis misses pipeline failure where generated finding anchors cannot be posted, causing incomplete or inconsistent final run.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full production path on a real test PR using the real model and GitHub API; assert autonomous completion plus consistency invariants (posted inline count/IDs, summary linkage, terminal success state) without operator fixes.
